title: psidium refractum
intro: Psidium refractum, a lesser-known species of the Myrtaceae family, is also known as the 'Guava-leaved West Indian cherry'. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Psidium refractum include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its high antioxidant and flavonoid content. Herbal preparations of Psidium refractum are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation in the body.
